Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Brian McMahon
Message:
Auth cutover to shared nousergon-auth identity service (phase 1)

Non-destructive first phase of vires-ops#60: Vires stops owning login and
delegates identity to the shared nousergon-auth service, while the legacy
magic-link/session/allowlist machinery stays fully functional until the new
path is verified live (phase 2 then drops it).

Backend:
- users.identity_user_id (nullable, unique, indexed) — additive migration
- current_identity() accepts `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` verified against
  the service's JWKS (pyjwt/EdDSA, cached — no per-request round-trip);
  resolution ladder: identity_user_id -> one-time email link -> JIT-provision.
  Replaces verify-time "always mint new" (root cause of vires-ops#57).
- A presented bearer is authoritative: invalid => 401, no cookie fallback.
- Same email linked to a DIFFERENT identity => loud 409, never a silent relink.

SPA:
- Sign-in via better-auth client against the shared service (invite-code
  field for new signups; metadata.product="vires")
- Short-lived identity JWT minted from the cross-subdomain session and sent
  as Bearer on every /api call (cached, 60s refresh margin, 30s backoff)
- /auth/verify page deleted — the shared service's verify endpoint sets the
  cookie and redirects itself
- Logout clears both the shared session and the legacy cookie

Part of vires-ops#60; merge is gated on nousergon-auth#1 (live deploy of
auth.nousergon.ai) — landing this before then would point login at a
not-yet-live service.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
